title: [Venusaur] Battle Guide
summary: A classic sun [sweeper] that rides [chlorophyll] to outrun the entire Champions metagame as a [Grass]/[Poison] special attacker.
weaknesses: Loses its Speed tier the moment sun is removed

recommendedMoves.reason: Main [Grass] STAB that keeps [Venusaur] healthy mid-sweep
[Poison] STAB to punish Fairies and other [Grass] types
Match-up theft and free turns for your setup partners

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Venusaur] is a [Grass]/[Poison] special attacker whose entire case hinges on [chlorophyll]. Its 80/100/100/80 line looks ordinary, but once sun is up its effective Speed clears 160 and outruns nearly every unboosted threat. The role is clean — sit in back, wait for sun, come in on turn two, break the front line.

## Strengths
[chlorophyll] and sun put [Venusaur] at the top of the Speed tier, and 100 SpA cracks neutral walls with [giga-drain] and [sludge-bomb]. The STAB combo hits Fairies, Waters, Grasses, and Grounds from useful angles, and [sleep-powder] steals awkward 1-on-1s for free turns.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ice], [Fire], [Flying], and [psychic] all hit for 2x, and losing the Speed race to any of them usually loses [Venusaur] outright. Sun management and Tera timing are the whole survival plan. Priority, [freeze-dry], and [stealth-rock] all compound the problem — every entry must be earned.

## Recommended Moveset
[giga-drain] is the locked STAB because the healing carries [Venusaur] through chip. [sludge-bomb] handles Fairies and mirror [Grass]. [earth-power] is the mandatory coverage that punishes [Poison] and [Steel] pivots trying to wall the STAB combo. The last slot is [sleep-powder] to convert shaky matchups into free setup turns for partners.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default — it guarantees one priority hit survived and a retaliation. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when sun uptime isn't guaranteed, preserving the Speed axis. [leftovers] fits the [leech-seed] / [sleep-powder] long-game build. The ability must be [chlorophyll]. Tera [Grass] maxes [giga-drain]; Tera [Fire] flips the [Ice] and [Fire] weaknesses.

## Teammates & Synergy
A dedicated sun setter (Ninetales is cleanest) is effectively mandatory. Speed control and [stealth-rock] leads reduce the pressure of getting [Venusaur] in cleanly, and any ally that sets up behind a [sleep-powder] turn snowballs fast. The team must also carry a dedicated answer to opposing Rain, Sand, or Snow leads.

## Play Patterns
Lead the sun setter, switch [Venusaur] in on turn two, never attack blind without sun. Save Tera for the moment an [Ice] or [Fire] move is telegraphed — that swap turns a 50/50 into a guaranteed tempo lead.
